15: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/toolname"
16: 	"github.com/jrswab/axe/internal/xdg"
17: )
18: 
19: // CallAgentToolName is the constant name for the sub-agent invocation tool.
20: const CallAgentToolName = toolname.CallAgent
21: 
22: // maxConversationTurns is the safety limit for the conversation loop.
23: const maxConversationTurns = 50
24: 
25: // ExecuteOptions holds configuration for executing a call_agent tool call.
26: type ExecuteOptions struct {
27: 	AllowedAgents []string
28: 	ParentModel   string
29: 	Depth         int
30: 	MaxDepth      int
31: 	Timeout       int
32: 	GlobalConfig  *config.GlobalConfig
33: 	Verbose       bool
34: 	Stderr        io.Writer
35: }
36: 
37: // CallAgentTool returns the call_agent tool definition for LLM tool calling.
38: // The allowedAgents list is included in the description and parameter descriptions
39: // so the LLM knows which agent names are valid.
40: func CallAgentTool(allowedAgents []string) provider.Tool {
41: 	agentList := strings.Join(allowedAgents, ", ")
42: 
43: 	return provider.Tool{
44: 		Name:        CallAgentToolName,
45: 		Description: "Delegate a task to a sub-agent. The sub-agent runs independently with its own context and returns only its final result. Available agents: " + agentList,
46: 		Parameters: map[string]provider.ToolParameter{
47: 			"agent": {
48: 				Type:        "string",
49: 				Description: "Name of the sub-agent to invoke (must be one of: " + agentList + ")",
50: 				Required:    true,
51: 			},
52: 			"task": {
53: 				Type:        "string",
54: 				Description: "What you need the sub-agent to do",
55: 				Required:    true,
56: 			},
57: 			"context": {
58: 				Type:        "string",
59: 				Description: "Additional context from your conversation to pass along",
60: 				Required:    false,
61: 			},
62: 		},
63: 	}
64: }
65: 
66: // ExecuteCallAgent executes a call_agent tool call by loading and running a sub-agent.
67: // It always returns a ToolResult (never an error). Errors are communicated via
68: // ToolResult.Content and ToolResult.IsError fields.
69: func ExecuteCallAgent(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, opts ExecuteOptions) provider.ToolResult {
70: 	// Step 1: Extract arguments
71: 	agentName := call.Arguments["agent"]
72: 	task := call.Arguments["task"]
73: 	taskContext := call.Arguments["context"]
74: 
75: 	// Step 2: Validate agent name
76: 	if agentName == "" {
77: 		return provider.ToolResult{
78: 			CallID:  call.ID,
79: 			Content: `call_agent error: "agent" argument is required`,
80: 			IsError: true,
81: 		}
82: 	}
83: 
84: 	// Step 3: Validate task
85: 	if task == "" {
86: 		return provider.ToolResult{
87: 			CallID:  call.ID,
88: 			Content: `call_agent error: "task" argument is required`,
89: 			IsError: true,
90: 		}
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	// Step 4: Validate agent is allowed
94: 	allowed := false
95: 	for _, a := range opts.AllowedAgents {
96: 		if a == agentName {
97: 			allowed = true
98: 			break
99: 		}
100: 	}
101: 	if !allowed {
102: 		return provider.ToolResult{
103: 			CallID:  call.ID,
104: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("call_agent error: agent %q is not in this agent's sub_agents list", agentName),
105: 			IsError: true,
106: 		}
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	// Step 5: Check depth limit
110: 	if opts.Depth >= opts.MaxDepth {
111: 		return provider.ToolResult{
112: 			CallID:  call.ID,
113: 			Content: fmt.Sprintf("call_agent error: maximum sub-agent depth (%d) reached", opts.MaxDepth),
114: 			IsError: true,
115: 		}
116: 	}
117: 
118: 	// Verbose: log before sub-agent call
119: 	if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
120: 		taskPreview := task
121: 		if len(taskPreview) > 80 {
122: 			taskPreview = taskPreview[:80] + "..."
123: 		}
124: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Calling %q (depth %d) with task: %s\n", agentName, opts.Depth+1, taskPreview)
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	start := time.Now()
128: 
129: 	// Step 6: Load sub-agent config
130: 	cfg, err := agent.Load(agentName)
131: 	if err != nil {
132: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to load agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	// Step 7: Parse sub-agent's model
136: 	provName, modelName, err := parseModel(cfg.Model)
137: 	if err != nil {
138: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("invalid model for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
139: 	}
140: 
141: 	// Step 8: Resolve sub-agent's working directory, files, skill, system prompt
142: 	workdir, err := resolve.Workdir("", cfg.Workdir)
143: 	if err != nil {
144: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to resolve workdir for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
145: 	}
146: 
147: 	files, err := resolve.Files(cfg.Files, workdir)
148: 	if err != nil {
149: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to resolve files for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	configDir, err := xdg.GetConfigDir()
153: 	if err != nil {
154: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to get config dir: %s", err), opts)
155: 	}
156: 
157: 	skillContent, err := resolve.Skill(cfg.Skill, configDir)
158: 	if err != nil {
159: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to load skill for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
160: 	}
161: 
162: 	systemPrompt := resolve.BuildSystemPrompt(cfg.SystemPrompt, skillContent, files)
163: 
164: 	// Step 8b: Memory — load entries into system prompt
165: 	if cfg.Memory.Enabled {
166: 		memPath, memErr := memory.FilePath(agentName, cfg.Memory.Path)
167: 		if memErr != nil {
168: 			if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
169: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to load memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, memErr)
170: 			}
171: 		} else {
172: 			entries, memErr := memory.LoadEntries(memPath, cfg.Memory.LastN)
173: 			if memErr != nil {
174: 				if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
175: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to load memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, memErr)
176: 				}
177: 			} else if entries != "" {
178: 				systemPrompt += "\n\n---\n\n## Memory\n\n" + entries
179: 			}
180: 
181: 			memCount, memErr := memory.CountEntries(memPath)
182: 			if memErr != nil {
183: 				if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
184: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to count memory entries for %q: %v\n", agentName, memErr)
185: 				}
186: 			} else if cfg.Memory.MaxEntries > 0 && memCount >= cfg.Memory.MaxEntries {
187: 				if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
188: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: agent %q memory has %d entries (max_entries: %d). Run 'axe gc %s' to trim.\n", agentName, memCount, cfg.Memory.MaxEntries, agentName)
189: 				}
190: 			}
191: 		}
192: 	}
193: 
194: 	// Step 9: Resolve API key and base URL
195: 	globalCfg := opts.GlobalConfig
196: 	if globalCfg == nil {
197: 		globalCfg = &config.GlobalConfig{}
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	apiKey := globalCfg.ResolveAPIKey(provName)
201: 	baseURL := globalCfg.ResolveBaseURL(provName)
202: 
203: 	if provider.Supported(provName) && provName != "ollama" && apiKey == "" {
204: 		envVar := config.APIKeyEnvVar(provName)
205: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("API key for provider %q is not configured (set %s or add to config.toml)", provName, envVar), opts)
206: 	}
207: 
208: 	// Step 10: Create provider
209: 	prov, err := provider.New(provName, apiKey, baseURL)
210: 	if err != nil {
211: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, fmt.Sprintf("failed to create provider for agent %q: %s", agentName, err), opts)
212: 	}
213: 
214: 	// Step 11: Build user message
215: 	var userMessage string
216: 	if strings.TrimSpace(taskContext) != "" {
217: 		userMessage = fmt.Sprintf("Task: %s\n\nContext:\n%s", task, taskContext)
218: 	} else {
219: 		userMessage = fmt.Sprintf("Task: %s", task)
220: 	}
221: 
222: 	// Step 12: Build request
223: 	req := &provider.Request{
224: 		Model:       modelName,
225: 		System:      systemPrompt,
226: 		Messages:    []provider.Message{{Role: "user", Content: userMessage}},
227: 		Temperature: cfg.Params.Temperature,
228: 		MaxTokens:   cfg.Params.MaxTokens,
229: 	}
230: 
231: 	// Inject tools if sub-agent has sub_agents and depth allows
232: 	newDepth := opts.Depth + 1
233: 	if len(cfg.SubAgents) > 0 && newDepth < opts.MaxDepth {
234: 		req.Tools = []provider.Tool{CallAgentTool(cfg.SubAgents)}
235: 	}
236: 
237: 	// Resolve configured tools for the sub-agent
238: 	registry := NewRegistry()
239: 	RegisterAll(registry)
240: 	if len(cfg.Tools) > 0 {
241: 		resolvedTools, resolveErr := registry.Resolve(cfg.Tools)
242: 		if resolveErr != nil {
243: 			return provider.ToolResult{
244: 				CallID:  call.ID,
245: 				Content: fmt.Sprintf("failed to resolve tools for agent %q: %s", agentName, resolveErr),
246: 				IsError: true,
247: 			}
248: 		}
249: 		req.Tools = append(req.Tools, resolvedTools...)
250: 	}
251: 
252: 	// Step 13: Create timeout context
253: 	var callCtx context.Context
254: 	var cancel context.CancelFunc
255: 	if opts.Timeout > 0 {
256: 		callCtx, cancel = context.WithTimeout(ctx, time.Duration(opts.Timeout)*time.Second)
257: 	} else {
258: 		callCtx, cancel = context.WithCancel(ctx)
259: 	}
260: 	defer cancel()
261: 
262: 	// Step 14: Run conversation loop (or single-shot if no tools)
263: 	resp, err := runConversationLoop(callCtx, prov, req, cfg, registry, newDepth, opts, workdir)
264: 	if err != nil {
265: 		durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
266: 		if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
267: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] %q failed: %s\n", agentName, err)
268: 		}
269: 		_ = durationMs
270: 		return errorResult(call.ID, agentName, err.Error(), opts)
271: 	}
272: 
273: 	// Step 14b: Memory — append entry after successful response
274: 	if cfg.Memory.Enabled {
275: 		appendPath, appendErr := memory.FilePath(agentName, cfg.Memory.Path)
276: 		if appendErr != nil {
277: 			if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
278: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to save memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, appendErr)
279: 			}
280: 		} else {
281: 			if appendErr = memory.AppendEntry(appendPath, userMessage, resp.Content); appendErr != nil {
282: 				if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
283: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] Warning: failed to save memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, appendErr)
284: 				}
285: 			}
286: 		}
287: 	}
288: 
289: 	// Step 15: Return result
290: 	durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
291: 	if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
292: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] %q completed in %dms (%d chars returned)\n", agentName, durationMs, len(resp.Content))
293: 	}
294: 
295: 	return provider.ToolResult{
296: 		CallID:  call.ID,
297: 		Content: resp.Content,
298: 		IsError: false,
299: 	}
300: }
301: 
302: // runConversationLoop runs the multi-turn conversation loop for a sub-agent.
303: // If the sub-agent has no tools, this is a single-shot call.
304: func runConversationLoop(ctx context.Context, prov provider.Provider, req *provider.Request, cfg *agent.AgentConfig, registry *Registry, depth int, opts ExecuteOptions, toolWorkdir string) (*provider.Response, error) {
305: 	for turn := 0; turn < maxConversationTurns; turn++ {
306: 		resp, err := prov.Send(ctx, req)
307: 		if err != nil {
308: 			return nil, err
309: 		}
310: 
311: 		// No tool calls: we're done
312: 		if len(resp.ToolCalls) == 0 {
313: 			return resp, nil
314: 		}
315: 
316: 		// If no tools were sent, treat as done even if response has tool calls
317: 		if len(req.Tools) == 0 {
318: 			return resp, nil
319: 		}
320: 
321: 		// Append assistant message with tool calls
322: 		assistantMsg := provider.Message{
323: 			Role:      "assistant",
324: 			Content:   resp.Content,
325: 			ToolCalls: resp.ToolCalls,
326: 		}
327: 		req.Messages = append(req.Messages, assistantMsg)
328: 
329: 		// Execute tool calls and collect results
330: 		results := make([]provider.ToolResult, len(resp.ToolCalls))
331: 		for i, tc := range resp.ToolCalls {
332: 			if tc.Name == CallAgentToolName {
333: 				subOpts := ExecuteOptions{
334: 					AllowedAgents: cfg.SubAgents,
335: 					ParentModel:   cfg.Model,
336: 					Depth:         depth,
337: 					MaxDepth:      opts.MaxDepth,
338: 					Timeout:       opts.Timeout,
339: 					GlobalConfig:  opts.GlobalConfig,
340: 					Verbose:       opts.Verbose,
341: 					Stderr:        opts.Stderr,
342: 				}
343: 				results[i] = ExecuteCallAgent(ctx, tc, subOpts)
344: 			} else {
345: 				execCtx := ExecContext{
346: 					Workdir: toolWorkdir,
347: 					Stderr:  opts.Stderr,
348: 					Verbose: opts.Verbose,
349: 				}
350: 				result, dispatchErr := registry.Dispatch(ctx, tc, execCtx)
351: 				if dispatchErr != nil {
352: 					results[i] = provider.ToolResult{
353: 						CallID:  tc.ID,
354: 						Content: dispatchErr.Error(),
355: 						IsError: true,
356: 					}
357: 				} else {
358: 					results[i] = result
359: 				}
360: 			}
361: 		}
362: 
363: 		// Append tool result message
364: 		toolMsg := provider.Message{
365: 			Role:        "tool",
366: 			ToolResults: results,
367: 		}
368: 		req.Messages = append(req.Messages, toolMsg)
369: 	}
370: 
371: 	return nil, fmt.Errorf("sub-agent exceeded maximum conversation turns (%d)", maxConversationTurns)
372: }
373: 
374: // errorResult creates an error ToolResult for a sub-agent failure.
375: func errorResult(callID, agentName, errMsg string, opts ExecuteOptions) provider.ToolResult {
376: 	if opts.Verbose && opts.Stderr != nil {
377: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Stderr, "[sub-agent] %q failed: %s\n", agentName, errMsg)
378: 	}
379: 	return provider.ToolResult{
380: 		CallID:  callID,
381: 		Content: fmt.Sprintf("Error: sub-agent %q failed - %s. You may retry or proceed without this result.", agentName, errMsg),
382: 		IsError: true,
383: 	}
384: }
385: 
386: // parseModel splits a "provider/model-name" string into provider and model parts.
387: func parseModel(model string) (providerName, modelName string, err error) {
388: 	idx := strings.Index(model, "/")
389: 	if idx < 0 {
390: 		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid model format %q: expected provider/model-name", model)
391: 	}
392: 
393: 	providerName = model[:idx]
394: 	modelName = model[idx+1:]
395: 
396: 	if providerName == "" {
397: 		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid model format %q: empty provider", model)
398: 	}
399: 	if modelName == "" {
400: 		return "", "", fmt.Errorf("invalid model format %q: empty model name", model)
401: 	}
402: 
403: 	return providerName, modelName, nil
404: }

(End of file - total 404 lines)
</content>

81: func truncateOutput(s string) string {
82: 	if len(s) <= maxToolOutputBytes {
83: 		return s
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	// Backtrack from the byte limit to avoid splitting a multi-byte UTF-8 rune.
87: 	i := maxToolOutputBytes

95: func runAgent(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
96: 	agentName := args[0]
97: 
98: 	// Step 1: Load agent config

100: 	if err != nil {

174: 	var memoryPath string

183: 			if memErr != nil {

274: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Model:    %s/%s\n", provName, modelName)

276: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Skill:    %s\n", skillDisplay)
277: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Files:    %d file(s)\n", len(files))
278: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Stdin:    %s\n", stdinDisplay)
279: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Timeout:  %ds\n", timeout)
280: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Params:   temperature=%g, max_tokens=%d\n", cfg.Params.Temperature, cfg.Params.MaxTokens)
281: 		if cfg.Memory.Enabled {
282: 			if memoryCount > 0 {
283: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Memory:   %d entries loaded from %s\n", memoryCount, memoryPath)
284: 			} else {
285: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Memory:   0 entries (no memory file)\n")
286: 			}
287: 		}
288: 	}
289: 
290: 	// Step 17: Create context with timeout
291: 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), time.Duration(timeout)*time.Second)
292: 	defer cancel()
293: 
294: 	// Step 18: Call provider (conversation loop when tools are present)
295: 	start := time.Now()
296: 
297: 	// Determine parallel execution setting.
298: 	// Default is true (per spec). Only false if explicitly set via TOML.
299: 	// Using *bool allows distinguishing "not set" (nil) from "set to false".
300: 	parallel := true
301: 	if cfg.SubAgentsConf.Parallel != nil {
302: 		parallel = *cfg.SubAgentsConf.Parallel
303: 	}
304: 
305: 	var resp *provider.Response
306: 	var totalInputTokens int
307: 	var totalOutputTokens int
308: 	var totalToolCalls int
309: 	var allToolCallDetails []toolCallDetail
310: 
311: 	if len(req.Tools) == 0 {
312: 		// Single-shot: no tools, no conversation loop (identical to M4)
313: 		resp, err = prov.Send(ctx, req)
314: 		if err != nil {
315: 			durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
316: 			if verbose {
317: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Duration: %dms\n", durationMs)
318: 			}
319: 			return mapProviderError(err)
320: 		}
321: 		totalInputTokens = resp.InputTokens
322: 		totalOutputTokens = resp.OutputTokens
323: 
324: 		if verbose {
325: 			durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
326: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Duration: %dms\n", durationMs)
327: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Tokens:   %d input, %d output\n", resp.InputTokens, resp.OutputTokens)
328: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Stop:     %s\n", resp.StopReason)
329: 		}
330: 	} else {
331: 		// Conversation loop: handle tool calls
332: 		for turn := 0; turn < maxConversationTurns; turn++ {
333: 			if verbose {
334: 				pendingToolCalls := 0
335: 				for _, m := range req.Messages {
336: 					if m.Role == "tool" {
337: 						pendingToolCalls += len(m.ToolResults)
338: 					}
339: 				}
340: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "[turn %d] Sending request (%d messages, %d tool calls pending)\n", turn+1, len(req.Messages), pendingToolCalls)
341: 			}
342: 
343: 			resp, err = prov.Send(ctx, req)
344: 			if err != nil {
345: 				durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
346: 				if verbose {
347: 					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Duration: %dms\n", durationMs)
348: 				}
349: 				return mapProviderError(err)
350: 			}
351: 
352: 			totalInputTokens += resp.InputTokens
353: 			totalOutputTokens += resp.OutputTokens
354: 
355: 			if verbose {
356: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "[turn %d] Received response: %s (%d tool calls)\n", turn+1, resp.StopReason, len(resp.ToolCalls))
357: 			}
358: 
359: 			// No tool calls: conversation is done
360: 			if len(resp.ToolCalls) == 0 {
361: 				break
362: 			}
363: 
364: 			// Append assistant message with tool calls
365: 			assistantMsg := provider.Message{
366: 				Role:      "assistant",
367: 				Content:   resp.Content,
368: 				ToolCalls: resp.ToolCalls,
369: 			}
370: 			req.Messages = append(req.Messages, assistantMsg)
371: 
372: 			// Execute tool calls
373: 			results := executeToolCalls(ctx, resp.ToolCalls, cfg, globalCfg, registry, depth, effectiveMaxDepth, parallel, verbose, cmd.ErrOrStderr(), workdir)
374: 			totalToolCalls += len(resp.ToolCalls)
375: 
376: 			if jsonOutput {
377: 				for i, tc := range resp.ToolCalls {
378: 					input := tc.Arguments
379: 					if input == nil {
380: 						input = map[string]string{}
381: 					}
382: 
383: 					allToolCallDetails = append(allToolCallDetails, toolCallDetail{
384: 						Name:    tc.Name,
385: 						Input:   input,
386: 						Output:  truncateOutput(results[i].Content),
387: 						IsError: results[i].IsError,
388: 					})
389: 				}
390: 			}
391: 
392: 			// Append tool result message
393: 			toolMsg := provider.Message{
394: 				Role:        "tool",
395: 				ToolResults: results,
396: 			}
397: 			req.Messages = append(req.Messages, toolMsg)
398: 		}
399: 
400: 		// Check if we exhausted turns
401: 		if resp != nil && len(resp.ToolCalls) > 0 {
402: 			return &ExitError{Code: 1, Err: fmt.Errorf("agent exceeded maximum conversation turns (%d)", maxConversationTurns)}
403: 		}
404: 
405: 		if verbose {
406: 			durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
407: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Duration: %dms\n", durationMs)
408: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Tokens:   %d input, %d output (cumulative)\n", totalInputTokens, totalOutputTokens)
409: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Stop:     %s\n", resp.StopReason)
410: 		}
411: 	}
412: 
413: 	durationMs := time.Since(start).Milliseconds()
414: 
415: 	// Step 19: JSON output
416: 	if jsonOutput {
417: 		if allToolCallDetails == nil {
418: 			allToolCallDetails = make([]toolCallDetail, 0)
419: 		}
420: 
421: 		envelope := map[string]interface{}{
422: 			"model":             resp.Model,
423: 			"content":           resp.Content,
424: 			"input_tokens":      totalInputTokens,
425: 			"output_tokens":     totalOutputTokens,
426: 			"stop_reason":       resp.StopReason,
427: 			"duration_ms":       durationMs,
428: 			"tool_calls":        totalToolCalls,
429: 			"tool_call_details": allToolCallDetails,
430: 			"refused":           refusal.Detect(resp.Content),
431: 		}
432: 		data, err := json.Marshal(envelope)
433: 		if err != nil {
434: 			return &ExitError{Code: 1, Err: fmt.Errorf("failed to marshal JSON output: %w", err)}
435: 		}
436: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(data))
437: 	} else {
438: 		// Step 20: Default output
439: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), resp.Content)
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	// Step 21: Append memory entry after successful response
443: 	if cfg.Memory.Enabled {
444: 		appendPath, appendErr := memory.FilePath(agentName, cfg.Memory.Path)
445: 		if appendErr != nil {
446: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Warning: failed to save memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, appendErr)
447: 		} else {
448: 			if appendErr = memory.AppendEntry(appendPath, userMessage, resp.Content); appendErr != nil {
449: 				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "Warning: failed to save memory for %q: %v\n", agentName, appendErr)
450: 			}
451: 		}
452: 	}
453: 
454: 	return nil
455: }

457: func printDryRun(cmd *cobra.Command, cfg *agent.AgentConfig, provName, modelName, workdir string, timeout int, systemPrompt, skillContent string, files []resolve.FileContent, stdinContent string, memoryEntries string) error {

459:

463: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "Workdir:  %s\n", workdir)
464: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "Timeout:  %ds\n", timeout)
465: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "Params:   temperature=%g, max_tokens=%d\n", cfg.Params.Temperature, cfg.Params.MaxTokens)
466: 
467: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
468: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "--- System Prompt ---")
469: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, systemPrompt)
470: 
471: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
472: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "--- Skill ---")
473: 	if skillContent != "" {
474: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, skillContent)
475: 	} else {
476: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "(none)")
477: 	}
478: 
479: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
480: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "--- Files (%d) ---\n", len(files))
481: 	if len(files) > 0 {
482: 		for _, f := range files {
483: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, f.Path)
484: 		}
485: 	} else {
486: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "(none)")
487: 	}
488: 
489: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
490: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "--- Stdin ---")
491: 	if strings.TrimSpace(stdinContent) != "" {
492: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, stdinContent)
493: 	} else {
494: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "(none)")
495: 	}
496: 
497: 	if cfg.Memory.Enabled {
498: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
499: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "--- Memory ---")
500: 		if memoryEntries != "" {
501: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, memoryEntries)
502: 		} else {
503: 			_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "(none)")
504: 		}
505: 	}
506: 
507: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
508: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "--- Tools ---")
509: 	if len(cfg.Tools) > 0 {
510: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, strings.Join(cfg.Tools, ", "))
511: 	} else {
512: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "(none)")
513: 	}
514: 
515: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out)
516: 	_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "--- Sub-Agents ---")
517: 	if len(cfg.SubAgents) > 0 {
518: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, strings.Join(cfg.SubAgents, ", "))
519: 		effectiveMaxDepth := 3
520: 		if cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth > 0 && cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth <= 5 {
521: 			effectiveMaxDepth = cfg.SubAgentsConf.MaxDepth
522: 		}
523: 		parallelVal := "yes"
524: 		if cfg.SubAgentsConf.Parallel != nil && !*cfg.SubAgentsConf.Parallel {
525: 			parallelVal = "no"
526: 		}
527: 		timeoutVal := cfg.SubAgentsConf.Timeout
528: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "Max Depth: %d\n", effectiveMaxDepth)
529: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "Parallel:  %s\n", parallelVal)
530: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(out, "Timeout:   %ds\n", timeoutVal)
531: 	} else {
532: 		_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, "(none)")
533: 	}
534: 
535: 	return nil
536: }

538: // executeToolCalls dispatches tool calls and returns results.
539: // When parallel is true and there are multiple calls, they run concurrently.
540: func executeToolCalls(ctx context.Context, toolCalls []provider.ToolCall, cfg *agent.AgentConfig, globalCfg *config.GlobalConfig, registry *tool.Registry, depth, maxDepth int, parallel, verbose bool, stderr io.Writer, workdir string) []provider.ToolResult {
541: 	results := make([]provider.ToolResult, len(toolCalls))
542: 
543: 	execOpts := tool.ExecuteOptions{
544: 		AllowedAgents: cfg.SubAgents,
545: 		ParentModel:   cfg.Model,
546: 		Depth:         depth,
547: 		MaxDepth:      maxDepth,
548: 		Timeout:       cfg.SubAgentsConf.Timeout,
549: 		GlobalConfig:  globalCfg,
550: 		Verbose:       verbose,
551: 		Stderr:        stderr,
552: 	}
553: 
554: 	if len(toolCalls) == 1 || !parallel {
555: 		// Sequential execution (also used for single call)
556: 		for i, tc := range toolCalls {
557: 			if tc.Name == tool.CallAgentToolName {
558: 				results[i] = tool.ExecuteCallAgent(ctx, tc, execOpts)
559: 			} else {
560: 				result, dispatchErr := registry.Dispatch(ctx, tc, tool.ExecContext{Workdir: workdir, Stderr: stderr, Verbose: verbose})
561: 				if dispatchErr != nil {
562: 					results[i] = provider.ToolResult{
563: 						CallID:  tc.ID,
564: 						Content: dispatchErr.Error(),
565: 						IsError: true,
566: 					}
567: 				} else {
568: 					results[i] = result
569: 				}
570: 			}
571: 		}
572: 	} else {
573: 		// Parallel execution
574: 		type indexedResult struct {
575: 			index  int
576: 			result provider.ToolResult
577: 		}
578: 		ch := make(chan indexedResult, len(toolCalls))
579: 		for i, tc := range toolCalls {
580: 			go func(idx int, call provider.ToolCall) {
581: 				var res provider.ToolResult
582: 				if call.Name == tool.CallAgentToolName {
583: 					res = tool.ExecuteCallAgent(ctx, call, execOpts)
584: 				} else {
585: 					result, dispatchErr := registry.Dispatch(ctx, call, tool.ExecContext{Workdir: workdir, Stderr: stderr, Verbose: verbose})
586: 					if dispatchErr != nil {
587: 						res = provider.ToolResult{
588: 							CallID:  call.ID,
589: 							Content: dispatchErr.Error(),
590: 							IsError: true,
591: 						}
592: 					} else {
593: 						res = result
594: 					}
595: 				}
596: 				ch <- indexedResult{index: idx, result: res}
597: 			}(i, tc)
598: 		}
599: 		for range toolCalls {
600: 			ir := <-ch
601: 			results[ir.index] = ir.result
602: 		}
603: 	}
604: 
605: 	return results
606: }
607: 
608: // mapProviderError converts a provider error to an ExitError with the correct exit code.
609: func mapProviderError(err error) error {
610: 	var provErr *provider.ProviderError
611: 	if errors.As(err, &provErr) {
612: 		switch provErr.Category {
613: 		case provider.ErrCategoryAuth, provider.ErrCategoryRateLimit,
614: 			provider.ErrCategoryTimeout, provider.ErrCategoryOverloaded,
615: 			provider.ErrCategoryServer:
616: 			return &ExitError{Code: 3, Err: provErr}
617: 		case provider.ErrCategoryBadRequest:
618: 			return &ExitError{Code: 1, Err: provErr}
619: 		}
620: 	}
621: 	return &ExitError{Code: 1, Err: err}
622: }

508: func TestTruncateOutput(t *testing.T) {
509: 	tests := []struct {
510: 		name        string
511: 		input       string
512: 		want        string
513: 		truncated   bool
514: 		wantLen     int
515: 		wantHasTail bool
516: 	}{
517: 		{
518: 			name:      "short unchanged",
519: 			input:     strings.Repeat("a", 5),
520: 			want:      strings.Repeat("a", 5),
521: 			truncated: false,
522: 			wantLen:   5,
523: 		},
524: 		{
525: 			name:      "exactly max unchanged",
526: 			input:     strings.Repeat("b", 1024),
527: 			want:      strings.Repeat("b", 1024),
528: 			truncated: false,
529: 			wantLen:   1024,
530: 		},
531: 		{
532: 			name:        "one over max truncated",
533: 			input:       strings.Repeat("c", 1025),
534: 			want:        strings.Repeat("c", 1024) + "... (truncated)",
535: 			truncated:   true,
536: 			wantLen:     1039,
537: 			wantHasTail: true,
538: 		},
539: 		{
540: 			name:        "long truncated",
541: 			input:       strings.Repeat("d", 2048),
542: 			want:        strings.Repeat("d", 1024) + "... (truncated)",
543: 			truncated:   true,
544: 			wantLen:     1039,
545: 			wantHasTail: true,
546: 		},
547: 		{
548: 			name:      "empty unchanged",
549: 			input:     "",
550: 			want:      "",
551: 			truncated: false,
552: 			wantLen:   0,
553: 		},
554: 	}
555: 
556: 	for _, tt := range tests {
557: 		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
558: 			got := truncateOutput(tt.input)
559: 			if got != tt.want {
560: 				t.Fatalf("truncateOutput() mismatch: got len=%d want len=%d", len(got), len(tt.want))
561: 			}
562: 			if len(got) != tt.wantLen {
563: 				t.Fatalf("truncateOutput() length mismatch: got=%d want=%d", len(got), tt.wantLen)
564: 			}
565: 			if tt.truncated != (len(tt.input) > 1024) {
566: 				t.Fatalf("invalid test case: truncated flag must match input length")
567: 			}
568: 			if tt.wantHasTail && !strings.HasSuffix(got, "... (truncated)") {
569: 				t.Fatalf("truncateOutput() missing truncation suffix")
570: 			}
571: 		})
572: 	}
573: }
574: 
575: func TestTruncateOutputUTF8(t *testing.T) {
576: 	const marker = "... (truncated)"
577: 
578: 	// "é" is 2 bytes (0xC3 0xA9). Fill 1023 bytes of ASCII then append "é"
579: 	// so the 2-byte rune straddles byte 1024. A naive slice at 1024 would
580: 	// split the rune; the fix must backtrack to 1023.
581: 	t.Run("2-byte rune at boundary", func(t *testing.T) {
582: 		input := strings.Repeat("a", 1023) + "é" // 1025 bytes total
583: 		got := truncateOutput(input)
584: 		if !utf8.ValidString(got) {
585: 			t.Fatalf("truncateOutput produced invalid UTF-8")
586: 		}
587: 		if !strings.HasSuffix(got, marker) {
588: 			t.Fatalf("missing truncation marker")
589: 		}
590: 		body := strings.TrimSuffix(got, marker)
591: 		if len(body) > maxToolOutputBytes {
592: 			t.Fatalf("body exceeds max bytes: got %d", len(body))
593: 		}
594: 	})
595: 
596: 	// "€" is 3 bytes (0xE2 0x82 0xAC). Fill 1022 ASCII bytes then "€"
597: 	// so byte 1024 lands on the second continuation byte.
598: 	t.Run("3-byte rune at boundary", func(t *testing.T) {
599: 		input := strings.Repeat("a", 1022) + "€" + strings.Repeat("a", 10) // 1035 bytes
600: 		got := truncateOutput(input)
601: 		if !utf8.ValidString(got) {
602: 			t.Fatalf("truncateOutput produced invalid UTF-8")
603: 		}
604: 		if !strings.HasSuffix(got, marker) {
605: 			t.Fatalf("missing truncation marker")
606: 		}
607: 		body := strings.TrimSuffix(got, marker)
608: 		if len(body) > maxToolOutputBytes {
609: 			t.Fatalf("body exceeds max bytes: got %d", len(body))
610: 		}
611: 	})
612: 
613: 	// "🔥" is 4 bytes (0xF0 0x9F 0x94 0xA5). Fill 1021 ASCII bytes then "🔥"
614: 	// so the rune spans bytes 1021-1024.
615: 	t.Run("4-byte rune at boundary", func(t *testing.T) {
616: 		input := strings.Repeat("a", 1021) + "🔥" + strings.Repeat("a", 10) // 1035 bytes
617: 		got := truncateOutput(input)
618: 		if !utf8.ValidString(got) {
619: 			t.Fatalf("truncateOutput produced invalid UTF-8")
620: 		}
621: 		if !strings.HasSuffix(got, marker) {
622: 			t.Fatalf("missing truncation marker")
623: 		}
624: 		body := strings.TrimSuffix(got, marker)
625: 		if len(body) > maxToolOutputBytes {
626: 			t.Fatalf("body exceeds max bytes: got %d", len(body))
627: 		}
628: 	})
629: 
630: 	// All multi-byte: 342 × "é" (2 bytes each) = 684 bytes, under limit.
631: 	t.Run("all multibyte under limit unchanged", func(t *testing.T) {
632: 		input := strings.Repeat("é", 342) // 684 bytes
633: 		got := truncateOutput(input)
634: 		if got != input {
635: 			t.Fatalf("expected unchanged output for input under limit")
636: 		}
637: 	})
638: }

640: func TestToolCallDetailJSON(t *testing.T) {
641: 	detail := toolCallDetail{
642: 		Name:    "read_file",
643: 		Input:   map[string]string{},
644: 		Output:  "content",
645: 		IsError: false,
646: 	}
647: 
648: 	data, err := json.Marshal(detail)
649: 	if err != nil {
650: 		t.Fatalf("marshal failed: %v", err)
651: 	}
652: 
653: 	var parsed map[string]interface{}
654: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &parsed); err != nil {
655: 		t.Fatalf("unmarshal failed: %v", err)
656: 	}
657: 
658: 	for _, key := range []string{"name", "input", "output", "is_error"} {
659: 		if _, ok := parsed[key]; !ok {
660: 			t.Fatalf("missing key %q in %s", key, string(data))
661: 		}
662: 	}
663: 
664: 	if isErr, ok := parsed["is_error"].(bool); !ok || isErr {
665: 		t.Fatalf("expected is_error=false, got %v", parsed["is_error"])
666: 	}
667: 
668: 	if inputRaw := parsed["input"]; inputRaw == nil {
669: 		t.Fatalf("expected input to be object, got null")
670: 	} else if inputMap, ok := inputRaw.(map[string]interface{}); !ok {
671: 		t.Fatalf("expected input to be object, got %T", inputRaw)
672: 	} else if len(inputMap) != 0 {
673: 		t.Fatalf("expected empty input map, got %v", inputMap)
674: 	}
675: 
676: 	withInput := toolCallDetail{
677: 		Name:    "read_file",
678: 		Input:   map[string]string{"path": "hello.txt", "mode": "full"},
679: 		Output:  "ok",
680: 		IsError: false,
681: 	}
682: 	data2, err := json.Marshal(withInput)
683: 	if err != nil {
684: 		t.Fatalf("marshal failed: %v", err)
685: 	}
686: 	var parsed2 map[string]interface{}
687: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(data2, &parsed2); err != nil {
688: 		t.Fatalf("unmarshal failed: %v", err)
689: 	}
690: 	inputMap2, ok := parsed2["input"].(map[string]interface{})
691: 	if !ok {
692: 		t.Fatalf("expected input object, got %T", parsed2["input"])
693: 	}
694: 	if inputMap2["path"] != "hello.txt" {
695: 		t.Fatalf("expected input.path=hello.txt, got %v", inputMap2["path"])
696: 	}
697: 	if inputMap2["mode"] != "full" {
698: 		t.Fatalf("expected input.mode=full, got %v", inputMap2["mode"])
699: 	}
700: }

1088: 		_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1089: 			"id": "msg_test",
1090: 			"type": "message",
1091: 			"role": "assistant",
1092: 			"content": [{"type": "text", "text": "No delegation needed."}],
1093: 			"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
1094: 			"stop_reason": "end_turn",
1095: 			"usage": {"input_tokens": 10, "output_tokens": 5}
1096: 		}`))

1099: 
1100: 	setupRunTestAgent(t, "parent-agent", `name = "parent-agent"
1101: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1102: sub_agents = ["helper"]
1103: `)

1111: 	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"run", "parent-agent"})
1112:

1114: 	if err != nil {
1115: 		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
1116: 	}

1119: 	if !strings.Contains(receivedBody, `"tools"`) {
1120: 		t.Errorf("expected 'tools' in request body, got %q", receivedBody)
1121: 	}
1122: 	if !strings.Contains(receivedBody, `"call_agent"`) {

1173: func TestRun_ConversationLoop_ToolCall(t *testing.T) {
1174: 	resetRunCmd(t)
1175: 
1176: 	// Parent server: first request returns a tool call, second returns text
1177: 	parentCallCount := 0
1178: 	parentServer := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
1179: 		parentCallCount++
1180: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
1181: 		if parentCallCount == 1 {
1182: 			// First call: return a tool_use calling "helper"
1183: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1184: 				"id": "msg_1",
1185: 				"type": "message",
1186: 				"role": "assistant",
1187: 				"content": [
1188: 					{"type": "text", "text": "Let me delegate this."},
1189: 					{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_123", "name": "call_agent", "input": {"agent": "helper", "task": "say hello"}}
1190: 				],
1191: 				"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
1192: 				"stop_reason": "tool_use",
1193: 				"usage": {"input_tokens": 10, "output_tokens": 20}
1194: 			}`))
1195: 		} else {
1196: 			// Second call: return final text
1197: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1198: 				"id": "msg_2",
1199: 				"type": "message",
1200: 				"role": "assistant",
1201: 				"content": [{"type": "text", "text": "The helper said: greetings!"}],
1202: 				"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
1203: 				"stop_reason": "end_turn",
1204: 				"usage": {"input_tokens": 30, "output_tokens": 10}
1205: 			}`))
1206: 		}
1207: 	}))
1208: 	defer parentServer.Close()
1209: 
1210: 	// Helper sub-agent server
1211: 	helperServer := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
1212: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
1213: 		_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1214: 			"id": "msg_helper",
1215: 			"type": "message",
1216: 			"role": "assistant",
1217: 			"content": [{"type": "text", "text": "greetings!"}],
1218: 			"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
1219: 			"stop_reason": "end_turn",
1220: 			"usage": {"input_tokens": 5, "output_tokens": 3}
1221: 		}`))
1222: 	}))
1223: 	defer helperServer.Close()
1224: 
1225: 	// Setup both agents - helper uses the same provider but different server
1226: 	tmpDir := setupRunTestAgent(t, "parent-loop", `name = "parent-loop"
1227: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1228: sub_agents = ["helper-loop"]
1229: `)
1230: 	// Create helper agent TOML
1231: 	agentsDir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "axe", "agents")
1232: 	_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(agentsDir, "helper-loop.toml"), []byte(`name = "helper-loop"
1233: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1234: `), 0644)
1235: 
1236: 	t.Setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "test-key")
1237: 	t.Setenv("AXE_ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", parentServer.URL)
1238: 
1239: 	buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
1240: 	errBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)
1241: 	rootCmd.SetOut(buf)
1242: 	rootCmd.SetErr(errBuf)
1243: 	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"run", "parent-loop"})
1244: 
1245: 	err := rootCmd.Execute()
1246: 	if err != nil {
1247: 		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
1248: 	}
1249: 
1250: 	output := buf.String()
1251: 	if !strings.Contains(output, "The helper said: greetings!") {
1252: 		t.Errorf("expected final response from parent, got %q", output)
1253: 	}
1254: 
1255: 	if parentCallCount != 2 {
1256: 		t.Errorf("expected parent server to be called 2 times, got %d", parentCallCount)
1257: 	}
1258: }

1260: func TestRun_ConversationLoop_MaxTurns(t *testing.T) {
1261: 	resetRunCmd(t)
1262:

1264: 	server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {

1266: 		_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{

1270: 			"content": [

1379: func TestRun_ParallelToolCalls(t *testing.T) {
1380: 	resetRunCmd(t)
1381: 
1382: 	// Track call counts and which agents were called
1383: 	var mu sync.Mutex
1384: 	callCount := 0
1385: 	subAgentCalls := make(map[string]bool)
1386: 
1387: 	server := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
1388: 		body := new(bytes.Buffer)
1389: 		_, _ = body.ReadFrom(r.Body)
1390: 		bodyStr := body.String()
1391: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
1392: 
1393: 		mu.Lock()
1394: 		callCount++
1395: 		currentCall := callCount
1396: 		mu.Unlock()
1397: 
1398: 		// Check if this is a sub-agent call (has "Task:" in the message)
1399: 		if strings.Contains(bodyStr, "Task: task for agent-a") {
1400: 			mu.Lock()
1401: 			subAgentCalls["agent-a"] = true
1402: 			mu.Unlock()
1403: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1404: 				"id": "msg_a", "type": "message", "role": "assistant",
1405: 				"content": [{"type": "text", "text": "result from a"}],
1406: 				"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "stop_reason": "end_turn",
1407: 				"usage": {"input_tokens": 5, "output_tokens": 3}
1408: 			}`))
1409: 			return
1410: 		}
1411: 		if strings.Contains(bodyStr, "Task: task for agent-b") {
1412: 			mu.Lock()
1413: 			subAgentCalls["agent-b"] = true
1414: 			mu.Unlock()
1415: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1416: 				"id": "msg_b", "type": "message", "role": "assistant",
1417: 				"content": [{"type": "text", "text": "result from b"}],
1418: 				"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "stop_reason": "end_turn",
1419: 				"usage": {"input_tokens": 5, "output_tokens": 3}
1420: 			}`))
1421: 			return
1422: 		}
1423: 
1424: 		// Parent calls
1425: 		if currentCall == 1 {
1426: 			// First parent call: return two tool calls
1427: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1428: 				"id": "msg_p1", "type": "message", "role": "assistant",
1429: 				"content": [
1430: 					{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_a", "name": "call_agent", "input": {"agent": "par-agent-a", "task": "task for agent-a"}},
1431: 					{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_b", "name": "call_agent", "input": {"agent": "par-agent-b", "task": "task for agent-b"}}
1432: 				],
1433: 				"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "stop_reason": "tool_use",
1434: 				"usage": {"input_tokens": 10, "output_tokens": 20}
1435: 			}`))
1436: 		} else {
1437: 			// Second parent call: final response
1438: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1439: 				"id": "msg_p2", "type": "message", "role": "assistant",
1440: 				"content": [{"type": "text", "text": "Both agents completed successfully."}],
1441: 				"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "stop_reason": "end_turn",
1442: 				"usage": {"input_tokens": 30, "output_tokens": 10}
1443: 			}`))
1444: 		}
1445: 	}))
1446: 	defer server.Close()
1447: 
1448: 	tmpDir := setupRunTestAgent(t, "par-parent", `name = "par-parent"
1449: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1450: sub_agents = ["par-agent-a", "par-agent-b"]
1451: 
1452: [sub_agents_config]
1453: parallel = true
1454: `)
1455: 	agentsDir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "axe", "agents")
1456: 	_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(agentsDir, "par-agent-a.toml"), []byte(`name = "par-agent-a"
1457: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1458: `), 0644)
1459: 	_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(agentsDir, "par-agent-b.toml"), []byte(`name = "par-agent-b"
1460: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1461: `), 0644)
1462: 
1463: 	t.Setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "test-key")
1464: 	t.Setenv("AXE_ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", server.URL)
1465: 
1466: 	buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
1467: 	errBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)
1468: 	rootCmd.SetOut(buf)
1469: 	rootCmd.SetErr(errBuf)
1470: 	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"run", "par-parent"})
1471: 
1472: 	err := rootCmd.Execute()
1473: 	if err != nil {
1474: 		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
1475: 	}
1476: 
1477: 	output := buf.String()
1478: 	if !strings.Contains(output, "Both agents completed successfully.") {
1479: 		t.Errorf("expected final response, got %q", output)
1480: 	}
1481: 
1482: 	mu.Lock()
1483: 	defer mu.Unlock()
1484: 	if !subAgentCalls["agent-a"] {
1485: 		t.Error("expected sub-agent 'agent-a' to be called")
1486: 	}
1487: 	if !subAgentCalls["agent-b"] {
1488: 		t.Error("expected sub-agent 'agent-b' to be called")
1489: 	}
1490: }

1492: func TestRun_SequentialToolCalls(t *testing.T) {

1523: 			mu.Lock()

1525: 			mu.Unlock()
1526: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{

1531: 			}`))
1532: 			return
1533: 		}
1534: 
1535: 		if currentCall == 1 {
1536: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1537: 				"id": "msg_p1", "type": "message", "role": "assistant",
1538: 				"content": [
1539: 					{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_a", "name": "call_agent", "input": {"agent": "seq-agent-a", "task": "seq task a"}},
1540: 					{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_b", "name": "call_agent", "input": {"agent": "seq-agent-b", "task": "seq task b"}}
1541: 				],
1542: 				"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "stop_reason": "tool_use",
1543: 				"usage": {"input_tokens": 10, "output_tokens": 20}
1544: 			}`))
1545: 		} else {
1546: 			_, _ = w.Write([]byte(`{
1547: 				"id": "msg_p2", "type": "message", "role": "assistant",
1548: 				"content": [{"type": "text", "text": "Sequential done."}],
1549: 				"model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "stop_reason": "end_turn",
1550: 				"usage": {"input_tokens": 30, "output_tokens": 10}
1551: 			}`))
1552: 		}
1553: 	}))
1554: 	defer server.Close()
1555: 
1556: 	tmpDir := setupRunTestAgent(t, "seq-parent", `name = "seq-parent"
1557: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1558: sub_agents = ["seq-agent-a", "seq-agent-b"]
1559: 
1560: [sub_agents_config]
1561: parallel = false
1562: `)
1563: 	agentsDir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "axe", "agents")
1564: 	_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(agentsDir, "seq-agent-a.toml"), []byte(`name = "seq-agent-a"
1565: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1566: `), 0644)
1567: 	_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(agentsDir, "seq-agent-b.toml"), []byte(`name = "seq-agent-b"
1568: model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
1569: `), 0644)

1571: 	t.Setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "test-key")
1572: 	t.Setenv("AXE_ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", server.URL)
1573:

1575: 	errBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)
1576: 	rootCmd.SetOut(buf)
1577: 	rootCmd.SetErr(errBuf)
1578: 	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"run", "seq-parent"})
1579: 
1580: 	err := rootCmd.Execute()
1581: 	if err != nil {
1582: 		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)

package cmd

import (
	"bytes"
	"encoding/json"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"sync"
	"testing"
	"time"
	"unicode/utf8"

 func TestRun_DryRunNoFiles(t *testing.T) {

+func TestRunCmd_DryRun_WithMCPServers(t *testing.T) {
+	resetRunCmd(t)
+	setupRunTestAgent(t, "dry-mcp", `name = "dry-mcp"
+model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
+
+[[mcp_servers]]
+name = "github"
+url = "https://mcp.example.com"
+transport = "streamable-http"
+`)
+
+	buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
+	errBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)
+	rootCmd.SetOut(buf)
+	rootCmd.SetErr(errBuf)
+	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"run", "dry-mcp", "--dry-run"})
+
+	err := rootCmd.Execute()
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	output := buf.String()
+	if !strings.Contains(output, "--- MCP Servers ---") {
+		t.Fatal("expected MCP servers section")
+	}
+	if !strings.Contains(output, "github: https://mcp.example.com (streamable-http)") {
+		t.Fatalf("expected configured MCP server line, got %q", output)
+	}
+}
+
+func TestRunCmd_DryRun_NoMCPServers(t *testing.T) {
+	resetRunCmd(t)
+	setupRunTestAgent(t, "dry-no-mcp", `name = "dry-no-mcp"
+model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
+`)
+
+	buf := new(bytes.Buffer)
+	errBuf := new(bytes.Buffer)
+	rootCmd.SetOut(buf)
+	rootCmd.SetErr(errBuf)
+	rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"run", "dry-no-mcp", "--dry-run"})
+
+	err := rootCmd.Execute()
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	output := buf.String()
+	if !strings.Contains(output, "--- MCP Servers ---") {
+		t.Fatal("expected MCP servers section")
+	}
+	if !strings.Contains(output, "--- MCP Servers ---\n(none)") {
+		t.Fatalf("expected MCP section to show (none), got %q", output)
+	}
+}

# Implementation Checklist: MCP (Model Context Protocol) Tool Support

**Spec:** `docs/plans/027_mcp_tool_support_spec.md`
**GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/9
**Tracking:** Check boxes as tasks are completed across sessions.

---

## Phase 1: SDK Dependency & Agent Config (no new packages yet)

These tasks modify existing files only. No MCP logic runs yet.

- [x] **1.1** Add `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` dependency to `go.mod`. Pin to a specific release version. Run `go mod tidy` to update `go.sum`.
  - Files: `go.mod`, `go.sum`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds.

- [x] **1.2** Add `MCPServerConfig` struct to `internal/agent/agent.go` (after line ~50). Fields: `Name string`, `URL string`, `Transport string`, `Headers map[string]string` with TOML tags per Requirement 1.1.
  - Files: `internal/agent/agent.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/agent/...` succeeds.

- [x] **1.3** Add `MCPServers []MCPServerConfig` field with tag `toml:"mcp_servers"` to `AgentConfig` struct at `internal/agent/agent.go:37-50`.
  - Files: `internal/agent/agent.go`
  - Verify: Existing `TestParseTOML_*` tests still pass. `make test` passes.

- [x] **1.4** Add MCP server validation logic to `Validate()` at `internal/agent/agent.go:55-84`. After existing field checks, iterate `MCPServers` and validate: non-empty `Name`, non-empty `URL`, `Transport` is `"sse"` or `"streamable-http"`, unique server names. Error messages per Requirements 2.2-2.3.
  - Files: `internal/agent/agent.go`
  - Verify: Existing tests pass. New tests (1.5) confirm behavior.

- [x] **1.5** Write agent config tests in `internal/agent/agent_test.go`:
  - `TestValidate_MCPServers_Valid` (Req 7.5)
  - `TestValidate_MCPServers_MissingName` (Req 7.5)
  - `TestValidate_MCPServers_MissingURL` (Req 7.5)
  - `TestValidate_MCPServers_InvalidTransport` (Req 7.5)
  - `TestValidate_MCPServers_ValidTransports` (Req 7.5)
  - `TestValidate_MCPServers_DuplicateNames` (Req 7.5)
  - `TestParseTOML_MCPServers` (Req 7.5)
  - `TestParseTOML_MCPServers_Empty` (Req 7.5)
  - Files: `internal/agent/agent_test.go`
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Red/green TDD: write tests first, confirm they fail, then confirm 1.4 makes them pass.

---

## Phase 2: Environment Variable Interpolation (`internal/envinterp/`)

New package, no integration yet. Fully testable in isolation.

- [x] **2.1** Create `internal/envinterp/envinterp.go`. Export `ExpandHeaders(headers map[string]string) (map[string]string, error)`. Implement regex-based `${VAR}` expansion per Requirements 3.1-3.7: nil input returns nil, empty map returns empty map, new map is returned (input unmodified), `os.Getenv` lookup, empty/unset vars return error.
  - Files: `internal/envinterp/envinterp.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/envinterp/...` succeeds.

- [x] **2.2** Write tests in `internal/envinterp/envinterp_test.go`. All 11 tests from Requirement 7.2:
  - `TestExpandHeaders_StaticValues`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_SingleVar`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_MultipleVarsInOneValue`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_MultipleHeaders`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_MissingVar`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_EmptyVar`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_NilMap`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_EmptyMap`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_NoPattern`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_DollarWithoutBrace`
  - `TestExpandHeaders_InputNotModified`
  - Files: `internal/envinterp/envinterp_test.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Red/green TDD.

---

## Phase 3: MCP Client (`internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go`)

New package. Wraps the MCP SDK. Testable with httptest MCP servers.

- [ ] **3.1** Create `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go`. Define `Client` struct holding server name and `*mcp.ClientSession`. Implement `Name() string` and `Close() error` methods per Requirements 4.2, 4.6, 4.7.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **3.2** Implement `Connect(ctx context.Context, cfg agent.MCPServerConfig) (*Client, error)` per Requirement 4.3. Steps: expand headers via `envinterp.ExpandHeaders`, create custom `RoundTripper` for header injection, create transport (`SSEClientTransport` or `StreamableClientTransport`), create `mcp.Client` with `{Name: "axe", Version: ...}`, call `client.Connect(ctx, transport)`, return wrapped `*Client`.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **3.3** Implement `(c *Client) ListTools(ctx context.Context) ([]provider.Tool, error)` per Requirement 4.4. Convert `mcp.Tool` to `provider.Tool` with type mapping: simple types (`string`, `integer`, `number`, `boolean`) map directly; complex types map to `"string"` with description prefix; missing type defaults to `"string"`; extract `required` array from top-level schema.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **3.4** Implement `(c *Client) CallTool(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall) (provider.ToolResult, error)` per Requirement 4.5. Convert `map[string]string` to `map[string]any`, call `session.CallTool`, extract `TextContent` items, concatenate with `"\n"`, handle no-text and error cases.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **3.5** Write tests in `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient_test.go` using real MCP test servers (SDK server utilities or httptest JSON-RPC). All tests from Requirement 7.3:
  - `TestConnect_StreamableHTTP_Success`
  - `TestConnect_InvalidTransport`
  - `TestConnect_UnreachableServer`
  - `TestConnect_HeaderInjection`
  - `TestConnect_HeaderEnvVarMissing`
  - `TestListTools_ReturnsTools`
  - `TestListTools_EmptyTools`
  - `TestListTools_ComplexSchema`
  - `TestCallTool_Success`
  - `TestCallTool_ToolError`
  - `TestCallTool_NoTextContent`
  - `TestCallTool_MultipleTextContent`
  - `TestClientName`
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient_test.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Red/green TDD.

---

## Phase 4: MCP Tool Router (`internal/mcpclient/router.go`)

Same package as client. Testable with real MCP test servers.

- [ ] **4.1** Create `internal/mcpclient/router.go`. Define `Router` struct mapping tool names to `*Client`. Implement `NewRouter() *Router` per Requirements 5.1-5.3.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **4.2** Implement `(r *Router) Register(client *Client, tools []provider.Tool, builtinNames map[string]bool) ([]provider.Tool, error)` per Requirement 5.4. Skip tools in `builtinNames`, error on MCP-to-MCP collision (with both server names in message), add remaining tools to map, return filtered slice.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **4.3** Implement `(r *Router) Dispatch(ctx, call) (provider.ToolResult, error)` per Requirement 5.5. Lookup client by `call.Name`, error if not found, delegate to `client.CallTool`.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **4.4** Implement `(r *Router) Has(name string) bool` per Requirement 5.6.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **4.5** Implement `(r *Router) Close() error` per Requirement 5.7. Close each unique client exactly once. Return first error.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [ ] **4.6** Write tests in `internal/mcpclient/router_test.go`. All tests from Requirement 7.4:
  - `TestRouter_RegisterAndDispatch`
  - `TestRouter_Has_UnknownTool`
  - `TestRouter_Dispatch_UnknownTool`
  - `TestRouter_Register_SkipsBuiltins`
  - `TestRouter_Register_MCPCollision`
  - `TestRouter_Close_ClosesAllClients`
  - `TestRouter_Close_DeduplicatesClients`
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router_test.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Red/green TDD.

---

## Phase 5: Integration into `cmd/run.go`

Wire MCP into the main run command. This is the largest integration phase.

- [ ] **5.1** Add `MCPRouter` field (type `*mcpclient.Router`, or an interface if needed) to `ExecuteOptions` in `internal/tool/tool.go:26-35` per Requirement 6.3. This field allows `runConversationLoop` and `ExecuteCallAgent` to access the parent's MCP router.
  - Files: `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [ ] **5.2** Update `runConversationLoop()` in `internal/tool/tool.go:304-372` to check `opts.MCPRouter` before dispatching to `registry.Dispatch()`. If `opts.MCPRouter != nil && opts.MCPRouter.Has(tc.Name)`, dispatch via `opts.MCPRouter.Dispatch(ctx, tc)` per Requirement 6.2.
  - Files: `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [ ] **5.3** Update `ExecuteCallAgent()` in `internal/tool/tool.go:69-299` to handle sub-agent MCP per Requirement 6.4. When a sub-agent has its own `cfg.MCPServers`, establish separate MCP connections, create its own router, discover tools, append to `req.Tools`, and defer `router.Close()`. The parent's router is NOT passed to sub-agents.
  - Files: `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [ ] **5.4** Add MCP server handling in `cmd/run.go` after tool registry resolution (~line 257). Per Requirement 6.1: if `cfg.MCPServers` is non-empty, create router, defer close, iterate servers, connect, list tools, build `builtinNames` from `cfg.Tools`, register with collision checks, append filtered tools to `req.Tools`. Map errors to correct `ExitError` codes (2 for config/env errors, 3 for connection/API errors).
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [ ] **5.5** Update `executeToolCalls()` in `cmd/run.go:540-606` to accept the router as a parameter per Requirement 6.2. Before falling through to `registry.Dispatch()`, check `router != nil && router.Has(tc.Name)` and dispatch via `router.Dispatch(ctx, tc)`.
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [ ] **5.6** Pass the router through all call sites of `executeToolCalls()` and into `ExecuteOptions` where needed. Ensure the router flows correctly through the conversation loop for the parent agent only.
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`, `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

---

## Phase 6: Verbose & Dry-Run Output

- [ ] **6.1** Add verbose logging for MCP in `cmd/run.go` per Requirement 8.1. Log to stderr with `[mcp]` prefix: before connecting, after tool discovery, on built-in skip. Use the `verbose` flag and `stderr` writer already available.

  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: Manual verification with `--verbose` flag.

- [ ] **6.3** Update `printDryRun()` in `cmd/run.go:457-536` to include `--- MCP Servers ---` section per Requirements 7.1-7.4. Place after `--- Tools ---` and before `--- Sub-Agents ---`. Print each server as `<name>: <url> (<transport>)` or `(none)` if empty. No MCP connections during dry-run.
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: `make test` passes with new dry-run tests (6.4).

- [ ] **6.4** Write dry-run tests in `cmd/run_test.go` per Requirement 7.6:
  - `TestRunCmd_DryRun_WithMCPServers` — fixture agent with MCP config, verify output contains `--- MCP Servers ---`, server name, URL, transport.
  - `TestRunCmd_DryRun_NoMCPServers` — existing fixture, verify output contains `--- MCP Servers ---` followed by `(none)`.
  - Create fixture TOML files in `cmd/testdata/agents/` as needed.
  - Files: `cmd/run_test.go`, `cmd/testdata/agents/*.toml` (new fixtures)
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Red/green TDD.

---

## Phase 7: Scaffold Update

- [ ] **7.1** Update `Scaffold()` in `internal/agent/agent.go:157-199` to include a commented-out MCP servers example per Requirement 9.1. Place after the `sub_agents_config` section and before the `[memory]` section. Use the exact TOML comment block from the spec.
  - Files: `internal/agent/agent.go`
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Manual verification of `axe new` output.

---

## Phase 8: JSON Output Verification

- [ ] **8.1** Verify MCP tool calls appear in `tool_call_details` array in `--json` output per Requirements 10.1-10.2. MCP tool calls use the same structure as built-in tool calls (`name`, `input`, `output`, `is_error`). The `tool_calls` count includes MCP calls. No new fields needed in the JSON envelope — confirm this works via the existing dispatch path.
  - Files: No changes expected (MCP calls go through the same `ToolResult` path). If any changes needed, update `cmd/run.go`.
  - Verify: Integration test or manual verification with `--json` flag against a real/test MCP server.

---

## Phase 9: Final Validation

- [ ] **9.1** Run `make test` and confirm all existing tests pass unchanged. All new tests pass. No regressions.
  - Verify: `make test` exits 0.

- [ ] **9.2** Confirm no changes to `internal/provider/provider.go` (Constraint 2).

- [ ] **9.3** Confirm no changes to `internal/toolname/toolname.go` (Constraint 3).

- [ ] **9.4** Confirm only one new external dependency: `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` (Constraint 1). No other new dependencies.

---

## Dependency Order

```
Phase 1 (config)
  |
Phase 2 (envinterp) ---- independent of Phase 1, but blocks Phase 3
  |
Phase 3 (mcpclient) ---- depends on Phase 1 (MCPServerConfig) and Phase 2 (envinterp)
  |
Phase 4 (router) -------- depends on Phase 3 (Client)
  |
Phase 5 (integration) --- depends on Phase 4 (Router) and Phase 1 (config)
  |
Phase 6 (output) -------- depends on Phase 5 (integration wiring)
  |
Phase 7 (scaffold) ------ independent, can run anytime after Phase 1
  |
Phase 8 (json verify) --- depends on Phase 5
  |
Phase 9 (final) --------- depends on all phases
```

Convert `mcp.Tool` to `provider.Tool` with type mapping: simple types (`string`, `integer`, `number`, `boolean`) map directly; complex types map to `"string"` with description prefix; missing type defaults to `"string"`; extract `required` array from top-level schema.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [x] **3.4** Implement `(c *Client) CallTool(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall) (provider.ToolResult, error)` per Requirement 4.5. Convert `map[string]string` to `map[string]any`, call `session.CallTool`, extract `TextContent` items, concatenate with `"\n"`, handle no-text and error cases.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [x] **3.5** Write tests in `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient_test.go` using real MCP test servers (SDK server utilities or httptest JSON-RPC). All tests from Requirement 7.3:
  - `TestConnect_StreamableHTTP_Success`
  - `TestConnect_InvalidTransport`
  - `TestConnect_UnreachableServer`
  - `TestConnect_HeaderInjection`
  - `TestConnect_HeaderEnvVarMissing`
  - `TestListTools_ReturnsTools`
  - `TestListTools_EmptyTools`
  - `TestListTools_ComplexSchema`
  - `TestCallTool_Success`
  - `TestCallTool_ToolError`
  - `TestCallTool_NoTextContent`
  - `TestCallTool_MultipleTextContent`
  - `TestClientName`
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient_test.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Red/green TDD.

---

## Phase 4: MCP Tool Router (`internal/mcpclient/router.go`)

Same package as client. Testable with real MCP test servers.

- [x] **4.1** Create `internal/mcpclient/router.go`. Define `Router` struct mapping tool names to `*Client`. Implement `NewRouter() *Router` per Requirements 5.1-5.3.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [x] **4.2** Implement `(r *Router) Register(client *Client, tools []provider.Tool, builtinNames map[string]bool) ([]provider.Tool, error)` per Requirement 5.4. Skip tools in `builtinNames`, error on MCP-to-MCP collision (with both server names in message), add remaining tools to map, return filtered slice.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [x] **4.3** Implement `(r *Router) Dispatch(ctx, call) (provider.ToolResult, error)` per Requirement 5.5. Lookup client by `call.Name`, error if not found, delegate to `client.CallTool`.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [x] **4.4** Implement `(r *Router) Has(name string) bool` per Requirement 5.6.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [x] **4.5** Implement `(r *Router) Close() error` per Requirement 5.7. Close each unique client exactly once. Return first error.
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./internal/mcpclient/...` succeeds.

- [x] **4.6** Write tests in `internal/mcpclient/router_test.go`. All tests from Requirement 7.4:
  - `TestRouter_RegisterAndDispatch`
  - `TestRouter_Has_UnknownTool`
  - `TestRouter_Dispatch_UnknownTool`
  - `TestRouter_Register_SkipsBuiltins`
  - `TestRouter_Register_MCPCollision`
  - `TestRouter_Close_ClosesAllClients`
  - `TestRouter_Close_DeduplicatesClients`
  - Files: `internal/mcpclient/router_test.go` (NEW)
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Red/green TDD.

---

## Phase 5: Integration into `cmd/run.go`

Wire MCP into the main run command. This is the largest integration phase.

- [x] **5.1** Add `MCPRouter` field (type `*mcpclient.Router`, or an interface if needed) to `ExecuteOptions` in `internal/tool/tool.go:26-35` per Requirement 6.3. This field allows `runConversationLoop` and `ExecuteCallAgent` to access the parent's MCP router.
  - Files: `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [x] **5.2** Update `runConversationLoop()` in `internal/tool/tool.go:304-372` to check `opts.MCPRouter` before dispatching to `registry.Dispatch()`. If `opts.MCPRouter != nil && opts.MCPRouter.Has(tc.Name)`, dispatch via `opts.MCPRouter.Dispatch(ctx, tc)` per Requirement 6.2.
  - Files: `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [x] **5.3** Update `ExecuteCallAgent()` in `internal/tool/tool.go:69-299` to handle sub-agent MCP per Requirement 6.4. When a sub-agent has its own `cfg.MCPServers`, establish separate MCP connections, create its own router, discover tools, append to `req.Tools`, and defer `router.Close()`. The parent's router is NOT passed to sub-agents.
  - Files: `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [x] **5.4** Add MCP server handling in `cmd/run.go` after tool registry resolution (~line 257). Per Requirement 6.1: if `cfg.MCPServers` is non-empty, create router, defer close, iterate servers, connect, list tools, build `builtinNames` from `cfg.Tools`, register with collision checks, append filtered tools to `req.Tools`. Map errors to correct `ExitError` codes (2 for config/env errors, 3 for connection/API errors).
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [x] **5.5** Update `executeToolCalls()` in `cmd/run.go:540-606` to accept the router as a parameter per Requirement 6.2. Before falling through to `registry.Dispatch()`, check `router != nil && router.Has(tc.Name)` and dispatch via `router.Dispatch(ctx, tc)`.
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

- [x] **5.6** Pass the router through all call sites of `executeToolCalls()` and into `ExecuteOptions` where needed. Ensure the router flows correctly through the conversation loop for the parent agent only.
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`, `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - Verify: `go build ./...` succeeds. Existing tests pass.

---

## Phase 6: Verbose & Dry-Run Output

- [x] **6.1** Add verbose logging for MCP in `cmd/run.go` per Requirement 8.1. Log to stderr with `[mcp]` prefix: before connecting, after tool discovery, on built-in skip. Use the `verbose` flag and `stderr` writer already available.
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: Manual verification with `--verbose` flag.

- [x] **6.2** Add verbose logging for MCP tool dispatch in `executeToolCalls()` per Requirement 8.1. Log `[mcp] Routing tool %q to server %q\n` before dispatching an MCP tool call.
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: Manual verification with `--verbose` flag.

- [x] **6.3** Update `printDryRun()` in `cmd/run.go:457-536` to include `--- MCP Servers ---` section per Requirements 7.1-7.4. Place after `--- Tools ---` and before `--- Sub-Agents ---`. Print each server as `<name>: <url> (<transport>)` or `(none)` if empty. No MCP connections during dry-run.
  - Files: `cmd/run.go`
  - Verify: `make test` passes with new dry-run tests (6.4).

- [x] **6.4** Write dry-run tests in `cmd/run_test.go` per Requirement 7.6:
  - `TestRunCmd_DryRun_WithMCPServers` — fixture agent with MCP config, verify output contains `--- MCP Servers ---`, server name, URL, transport.
  - `TestRunCmd_DryRun_NoMCPServers` — existing fixture, verify output contains `--- MCP Servers ---` followed by `(none)`.
  - Create fixture TOML files in `cmd/testdata/agents/` as needed.
  - Files: `cmd/run_test.go`, `cmd/testdata/agents/*.toml` (new fixtures)
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Red/green TDD.

---

## Phase 7: Scaffold Update

- [x] **7.1** Update `Scaffold()` in `internal/agent/agent.go:157-199` to include a commented-out MCP servers example per Requirement 9.1. Place after the `sub_agents_config` section and before the `[memory]` section. Use the exact TOML comment block from the spec.
  - Files: `internal/agent/agent.go`
  - Verify: `make test` passes. Manual verification of `axe new` output.

---

## Phase 8: JSON Output Verification

- [x] **8.1** Verify MCP tool calls appear in `tool_call_details` array in `--json` output per Requirements 10.1-10.2. MCP tool calls use the same structure as built-in tool calls (`name`, `input`, `output`, `is_error`). The `tool_calls` count includes MCP calls. No new fields needed in the JSON envelope — confirm this works via the existing dispatch path.
  - Files: No changes expected (MCP calls go through the same `ToolResult` path). If any changes needed, update `cmd/run.go`.
  - Verify: Integration test or manual verification with `--json` flag against a real/test MCP server.

---

## Phase 9: Final Validation

- [x] **9.1** Run `make test` and confirm all existing tests pass unchanged. All new tests pass. No regressions.
  - Verify: `make test` exits 0.

- [x] **9.2** Confirm no changes to `internal/provider/provider.go` (Constraint 2).

- [x] **9.3** Confirm no changes to `internal/toolname/toolname.go` (Constraint 3).

- [x] **9.4** Confirm only one new external dependency: `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` (Constraint 1). No other new dependencies.

- [ ] **9.5** Manual smoke test: create an agent TOML with `[[mcp_servers]]` pointing to a real or local MCP server. Run agent. Verify tools are discovered, tool calls are dispatched, results are returned. Verify `--dry-run`, `--verbose`, and `--json` output.

---

## Dependency Order

```
Phase 1 (config)
  |
Phase 2 (envinterp) ---- independent of Phase 1, but blocks Phase 3
  |
Phase 3 (mcpclient) ---- depends on Phase 1 (MCPServerConfig) and Phase 2 (envinterp)
  |
Phase 4 (router) -------- depends on Phase 3 (Client)
  |
Phase 5 (integration) --- depends on Phase 4 (Router) and Phase 1 (config)
  |
Phase 6 (output) -------- depends on Phase 5 (integration wiring)
  |
Phase 7 (scaffold) ------ independent, can run anytime after Phase 1
  |
Phase 8 (json verify) --- depends on Phase 5
  |
Phase 9 (final) --------- depends on all phases
```

input.prompt: Single task: review current working tree changes in /Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe including modified and untracked files for critical correctness issues only. Return 'no critical issues' or list critical issues with file/line and fix suggestion.

task_id: ses_33ea833dbffe2E3sNlAyqnCVPm (for resuming to continue this task if needed)

<task_result>
Critical issues found:

- `cmd/run.go:275` and `cmd/run.go:347` — `--timeout` is applied to two separate contexts (`mcpCtx` then main `ctx`), so MCP connect/list can consume up to `timeout` seconds *and* the conversation can consume another full `timeout`. This breaks the expected single overall deadline and can cause runs to exceed timeout significantly.  
  Fix suggestion: create one root timeout context once (before MCP setup), reuse it for MCP connect/list and provider/tool loop, and remove the second `WithTimeout`.

- `cmd/run.go:624` / `cmd/run.go:640` and `internal/tool/tool.go:370` — dispatch checks `tc.Name == call_agent` before checking MCP routing. If an MCP server exposes a tool named `call_agent`, calls are incorrectly routed to internal sub-agent execution instead of MCP, causing wrong behavior/failures.  
  Fix suggestion: route MCP tools first (`if router.Has(tc.Name) { ... }`) and only execute internal `call_agent` when the router does not own that name.
</task_result>

parts.text:
Implemented a full MCP integration pass for plan 027, then stopped exactly where your rule requires.

- Added MCP agent config support and validation in `internal/agent/agent.go`, plus coverage in `internal/agent/agent_test.go`.
- Added env header interpolation package with tests: `internal/envinterp/envinterp.go`, `internal/envinterp/envinterp_test.go`.
- Added MCP client + router packages and tests: `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient.go`, `internal/mcpclient/mcpclient_test.go`, `internal/mcpclient/router.go`, `internal/mcpclient/router_test.go`.
- Wired MCP routing into runtime execution paths:
  - parent run flow in `cmd/run.go`
  - sub-agent execution flow in `internal/tool/tool.go`
  - dry-run output MCP section + tests in `cmd/run.go`, `cmd/run_test.go`
  - updated dry-run goldens under `cmd/testdata/golden/dry-run/`
- Added scaffold MCP example block in `internal/agent/agent.go`.
- Added MCP SDK dependency in `go.mod`/`go.sum` (`github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk v1.4.0`).
- Updated implementation checklist tracking in `docs/plans/027_mcp_tool_support_implement.md`.

Validation run:
- `make test`: no failures
- `golangci-lint run`: `0 issues`

Per your hard stop rule, I ran `git diff --shortstat` after review/fixes and got:
- `13 files changed, 511 insertions(+), 57 deletions(-)`

That exceeds 500 total line changes, so I’m stopping implementation here.
